rolling_30d.summary: Zig-Zag experienced a severe MoM traffic contraction of -79.6% (9,591 sessions), though engagement rate improved +15.3 pp to 23.3%, suggesting a more qualified residual audience. The YoY picture is starkly different: sessions grew +157.1%, with mainsite_clicks (+52.0%), shop_now (+62.8%), and outbound_clicks (+26.3%) all accelerating YoY, indicating strong year-over-year momentum despite the recent monthly pullback. Direct traffic collapsed -86.4% MoM but soared +622.7% YoY, dominating 61.3% of all sessions; Organic Search remained stable MoM (-0.5%) and grew +25.3% YoY. YTD 2026 shows exceptional growth at 79,967 sessions (+636.3% vs YTD 2025), driven by a explosive February 2026 (+1443.1% YoY). Location-specific landing pages (/texas/lamesa/802-n-4th-st, /virginia/madison-heights/167-madison-heights-sq) show strong engagement rates (55.9%, 55.6%) and MoM growth, suggesting geo-targeted campaigns are resonating. However, store_locate (-17.0% MoM, -99.6% YoY) and locate_me (-17.2% MoM, -64.3% YoY) are in sharp decline, indicating a potential friction point or campaign shift away from location discovery.

quick_wins.title: Location-Based Landing Pages Driving High Engagement
quick_wins.detail: /texas/lamesa/802-n-4th-st and /virginia/madison-heights/167-madison-heights-sq are each delivering 55%+ engagement rates and MoM growth (+61.9% and new page respectively), signaling strong product-market fit for geo-targeted traffic.

quick_wins.title: Mainsite Clicks Remain Strong Performer
quick_wins.detail: 1,941 mainsite_clicks events (+52.0% YoY) demonstrate sustained high-funnel engagement, suggesting successful navigation to deeper conversion paths on the main domain.

quick_wins.title: Shop Now Events Climbing YoY
quick_wins.detail: 1,239 shop_now events show +62.8% YoY growth with stable MoM performance, indicating consistent e-commerce intent and revenue readiness among the visitor base.

potential_warnings.title: Severe Session Volume Drop MoM
potential_warnings.detail: Sessions fell -79.6% MoM to 9,591, driven primarily by Direct traffic collapsing -86.4%; this warrants immediate investigation into campaign pauses, budget cuts, or tracking changes that may have triggered the decline.

potential_warnings.title: Location Discovery Tools in Freefall
potential_warnings.detail: store_locate dropped -17.0% MoM and -99.6% YoY (132 events); locate_me fell -17.2% MoM and -64.3% YoY (106 events), suggesting either a campaign deprioritization or that users are finding locations through alternative means (e.g., get_directions +28.3% YoY).

potential_warnings.title: Homepage Engagement Declining YoY Despite High Volume
potential_warnings.detail: The homepage (/) remains the top landing page (535 sessions) but engagement is down -45.2% YoY with a 37.8% engagement rate, indicating possible messaging decay or UX friction relative to last year.

biggest_opportunities.title: Revive Location Finder Functionality
biggest_opportunities.detail: store_locate and locate_me have tanked YoY (-99.6% and -64.3%); audit whether the feature is broken, hidden, or if users are relying on get_directions instead (+28.3% YoY). Restoring visibility and usability could recover high-intent traffic.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale Geo-Targeted Landing Pages
biggest_opportunities.detail: Location-specific pages like /texas/lamesa/ and /virginia/madison-heights/ are outperforming the homepage on engagement; expand this template across the remaining store locations to replicate the 55%+ engagement lift.

biggest_opportunities.title: Investigate Direct Traffic Rebound Strategy
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct traffic is the lifeblood (+622.7% YoY, 61.3% of total) but dropped -86.4% MoM; validate that no tracking changes occurred and consider brand awareness campaigns to stabilize this critical channel.

biggest_opportunities.title: Optimize Homepage for Higher Engagement
biggest_opportunities.detail: The homepage receives 535 sessions but only 37.8% engagement and is down -45.2% YoY; A/B test messaging, CTA clarity, and page speed to recover lost engagement and reduce friction to conversion events.

last_calendar_month.summary: March 2026 delivered a sharp traffic reversal after two consecutive months of explosive growth, with sessions declining 73.0% MoM to 11,912—though YoY growth remains strong at +214.0%. Direct traffic collapsed 80.0% MoM but still sits +879.8% YoY, suggesting either a seasonal pullback or potential tracking/attribution issues that warrant investigation. Engagement quality actually improved, with engagement rate climbing 12.1 pp MoM to 20.1%, indicating that remaining traffic is more qualified. High-value conversions show resilience: mainsite_clicks grew 9.2% MoM (+51.2% YoY) and shop_now surged 8.2% MoM (+61.2% YoY), demonstrating strong downstream funnel momentum despite traffic headwinds. Location-based events (store_locate, locate_me) declined significantly MoM and YoY, hinting at weakening local discovery intent. YTD 2026 sits at 79,967 sessions (+636.3% vs YTD 2025), but the steep March decline raises questions about sustainability and whether February's 1,443.1% YoY spike represented a one-time anomaly or sustainable growth.

quick_wins.title: Mainsite clicks momentum persists despite traffic drop
quick_wins.detail: Mainsite_clicks grew 9.2% MoM and 51.2% YoY despite 73% session decline, indicating increasingly efficient conversion funnel and strong brand pull-through to primary domain.

quick_wins.title: Engagement rate rebounded 12.1 pp MoM
quick_wins.detail: With engagement rate climbing to 20.1% in March vs. 20.1% in February, the quality of remaining sessions has strengthened—engaged sessions held relatively stable at 2,389 despite 73% traffic loss.

quick_wins.title: Shop_now conversions sustain growth trajectory
quick_wins.detail: Shop_now events rose 8.2% MoM and 61.2% YoY to 1,270, securing Zig-Zag's second-highest event volume and demonstrating consistent e-commerce intent across traffic volatility.

quick_wins.title: Organic Search stability in volatile month
quick_wins.detail: Organic Search grew 8.1% MoM to 3,758 sessions (+26.7% YoY) and now represents 31.5% of traffic, providing a more reliable foundation as direct traffic stabilizes.

potential_warnings.title: Direct traffic collapse raises attribution red flags
potential_warnings.detail: Direct dropped 80.0% MoM from ~40,760 to 8,152 sessions—a dramatic reversal that mirrors overall session decline but exceeds it, suggesting possible tracking loss, campaign pause, or iOS privacy impact.

potential_warnings.title: Location-based intent cratering
potential_warnings.detail: Store_locate fell 2.1% MoM and -99.6% YoY to 143 events; locate_me dropped 5.0% MoM and -62.3% YoY to 113 events—critical for a local business network, indicating either reduced foot-traffic intent or potential feature deprecation.

potential_warnings.title: Homepage engagement collapse YoY
potential_warnings.detail: Homepage (/) generated 551 sessions but engagement rate fell 45.2% YoY, suggesting degrading landing page quality, UX friction, or competitive pressure on branded search.

potential_warnings.title: February spike may have been anomaly
potential_warnings.detail: February 2026 hit 44,086 sessions (+1,443.1% YoY), but March reverted to 11,912—a 73% drop that suggests February was driven by a one-time event, campaign, or data artifact rather than sustainable organic growth.

biggest_opportunities.title: Resurrect location finder functionality
biggest_opportunities.detail: Store_locate and locate_me events are down 60%+ YoY; rebuilding mobile-first location discovery (store_locate landing pages like /texas/lamesa/802-n-4th-st show 55.6% engagement) could unlock high-intent traffic for a network business.

biggest_opportunities.title: Scale high-performing location pages
biggest_opportunities.detail: Location landing pages like /texas/lamesa/802-n-4th-st (55.6% engagement, +89.5% MoM) and /ohio/carrollton/701-canton-rd-nw (56.7% engagement) drastically outperform homepage; prioritize SEO, paid, and internal linking to these high-intent pages.

biggest_opportunities.title: Stabilize and reactivate direct traffic channel
biggest_opportunities.detail: Direct fell 80% MoM but remains 68.4% of traffic; investigate tracking, verify GA4 configuration, audit recent campaign/email changes, and implement UTM hygiene to restore visibility and reliability.

biggest_opportunities.title: Expand outbound_clicks strategy
biggest_opportunities.detail: Outbound_clicks grew 14.0% MoM and 24.7% YoY to 2,710 events (highest event volume); these partnerships/integrations are working—analyze which referral partners drive highest ROI and allocate more budget/visibility.
